source.title: Testing predictions from the memory and identity theory of ICD-11 complex posttraumatic stress disorder

companion_theory_paper.title: The Memory and Identity Theory of ICD-11 Complex Posttraumatic Stress Disorder

extracted_concepts.name: Storage Strength vs Retrieval Strength
extracted_concepts.origin: Bjork & Bjork (1992) new theory of disuse
extracted_concepts.description: Storage strength (encoding intensity, how well a memory was laid down) and retrieval strength (current accessibility, how easily it can be found NOW) are independent dimensions. A deeply encoded memory can be inaccessible; a shallow memory can be highly accessible.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Current veld importance conflates both. The M&I theory explicitly separates them: storage strength is permanent (only grows), retrieval strength fluctuates with recency, context match, and access frequency.

extracted_concepts.name: Dual Representation (VAM/SAM)
extracted_concepts.origin: Brewin et al. (1996) dual representation theory, revised in Brewin et al. (2010)
extracted_concepts.description: Two parallel memory systems: VAMs (Verbally Accessible Memories - contextual, deliberate, hippocampal) and SAMs (Situationally Accessible Memories - sensation-based, automatic, amygdala-driven). They coexist for the same event but serve different functions.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Maps to semantic retrieval (deliberate, contextualized) vs graph/associative retrieval (automatic, cue-driven). The M&I theory says pathology arises from IMBALANCE between these - when sensation-based dominates contextual. In our system, imbalance between graph and semantic signals may cause retrieval failures.

extracted_concepts.name: Broad vs Specific Causal Pathways
extracted_concepts.origin: Hyland et al. (2024) SEM results - Table 4
extracted_concepts.description: Trauma memories predict ALL symptom clusters broadly (beta=0.40-0.63). Specific identity factors predict only SPECIFIC clusters: Vulnerable->avoidance+threat, Inferior->negative self-concept, Disconnected->disturbed relationships.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Semantic similarity is a broad retrieval signal (like trauma memories predicting everything). Graph/entity/ontological signals should be narrow and targeted (like specific identities predicting specific clusters). Currently we blend uniformly.

extracted_concepts.name: Four Identity Factors
extracted_concepts.origin: Hyland et al. (2024) EFA with N=2050, four-factor solution
extracted_concepts.description: Seven theoretical identities (unsafe, powerless, inferior, betrayed/abandoned, alienated, fragmented, non-existent) collapse into four orthogonal factors: Vulnerable (unsafe+powerless), Inferior, Disconnected (betrayed/abandoned+alienated), Fragmented (fragmented+non-existent).
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Memories have analogous health dimensions: Uncertain (low confidence), Low-value (low importance), Isolated (low graph connectivity), Incomplete (partial/fragmented content). These are orthogonal and affect retrieval and lifecycle differently.

extracted_concepts.name: Retrieval Competition
extracted_concepts.origin: Bjork & Bjork (1992), Brewin (2006) retrieval competition account
extracted_concepts.description: Multiple identities compete for activation. The primary determinant of which identity is experienced is the match between that identity and current circumstances. Identities with greater retrieval strength (more frequently/recently activated) are more accessible.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: After retrieval, memories should actively COMPETE against each other for coherence with the query context, not just be passively ranked by score. Contradicting memories about the same entity should resolve - the more recently accessed/confirmed one wins.

extracted_concepts.name: Pre-reflective to Self-aware Continuum
extracted_concepts.origin: Prebble et al. (2013), central to M&I theory structure
extracted_concepts.description: Experience exists on a continuum from pre-reflective (automatic, cue-driven, no metacognition, spatially/temporally bound) to self-aware (deliberate, contextualized, metacognitive, integrative).
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Maps to proactive_context (automatic, hook-driven, should be broad/associative) vs recall (deliberate, user-initiated, should be precise/targeted). Different retrieval strategies for different modes.

extracted_concepts.name: Bidirectional Memory-Identity Coupling
extracted_concepts.description: Memories trigger identities (intrusive memories -> negative self-perception). Identities trigger memories (negative identity activated -> serves as internal reminder -> precipitates reliving). The direction matters for which symptoms arise.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Graph edges should track directionality. A->B co-activation is different from B->A. When memory A frequently triggers recall of memory B but not vice versa, the edge should reflect this asymmetry.

extracted_concepts.name: Suppressor Effects in Multivariate Models
extracted_concepts.origin: Hyland et al. (2024) Table 4 - Inferior identity beta coefficients
extracted_concepts.description: Inferior identity had positive zero-order correlation with avoidance and affective dysregulation, but NEGATIVE regression coefficients (beta=-0.21 and beta=-0.15) in the full SEM. These are suppressor effects - artifacts of multivariate combination.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Multi-signal reranking in our system can produce analogous sign flips. A memory that ranks high on semantic similarity but low on graph activation may flip ranking direction when signals are combined. Should be detected and monitored.

extracted_concepts.name: Specificity of Non-prediction
extracted_concepts.origin: Hyland et al. (2024) - no identity predicted re-experiencing; only memory did
extracted_concepts.description: The M&I theory makes deliberate NON-predictions: identity disturbances should NOT predict re-experiencing symptoms. This was confirmed - no identity factor was significant for re-experiencing. This validates the structural distinction between memory and identity pathways.
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Some query types should be EXCLUSIVELY served by certain retrieval pathways. Factual attribute queries should NOT use graph spreading (it adds noise). Temporal queries should NOT rely on semantic similarity alone. The system should know what NOT to do.

extracted_concepts.name: Variance Partitioning Pattern
extracted_concepts.origin: Hyland et al. (2024) - R-squared values per symptom cluster
extracted_concepts.description: Trauma memories explain 38-58% of variance across all clusters. Specific identities add 0-20% incremental variance for their target clusters. Total R-squared ranges from 38.4% (avoidance) to 57.5% (affective dysregulation).
extracted_concepts.key_insight: Validates current hybrid weights (~50% semantic) as reasonable. Semantic similarity is the broad foundation; graph/identity signals add targeted incremental value. But the current fixed 50/35/15 split should vary by query type.

new_edges.name: Storage Strength / Retrieval Strength Interface
new_edges.current_state: importance() in types.rs is a single f32 conflating encoding quality and current accessibility
new_edges.new_boundary: Split into storage_strength (set at ingest, grows with Hebbian, never decays temporally) and retrieval_strength (access_count * recency * context_match)
new_edges.data_crossing: storage_strength flows into consolidation/decay; retrieval_strength flows into ranking. Currently conflated in calculate_temporal_relevance() at recall.rs:3605
new_edges.failure_mode: High-importance 91+ day memories get importance*0.2, effectively buried despite high encoding quality

new_edges.name: Broad Pathway / Specific Pathway Interface
new_edges.new_boundary: Query classification should shift weights: Attribute/Factual -> (20/40/40), Semantic -> (60/25/15), Temporal -> (35/30/35)
new_edges.data_crossing: classify_query() returns QueryType but this never reaches spreading_activation_retrieve_with_stats()
new_edges.failure_mode: Attribute queries like 'What is X's job?' get 50% semantic weight but 'job' and 'software engineer' have low cosine similarity

new_edges.name: Automatic / Deliberate Retrieval Interface
new_edges.current_state: proactive_context and recall both call semantic_retrieve with identical pipeline
new_edges.new_boundary: Automatic mode: broader spreading, lower thresholds, no cross-encoder. Deliberate mode: tight thresholds, cross-encoder enabled
new_edges.data_crossing: Query struct needs retrieval_mode field; proactive_context sets Automatic, explicit recall sets Deliberate
new_edges.failure_mode: proactive_context returns precision-optimized results, missing the broader associative connections that make proactive suggestions valuable

new_edges.name: Memory Competition Boundary
new_edges.current_state: After scoring, memories sorted by score and truncated. No inter-memory coherence check
new_edges.new_boundary: Top-K memories undergo coherence resolution - contradictory memories compete, winner survives
new_edges.data_crossing: CONFLICT_COSINE_THRESHOLD exists but only used at ingest, not retrieval
new_edges.failure_mode: Returns both 'Alice moved to NYC' and 'Alice lives in SF' without resolution

coherent_fractals.name: Dual Representation
coherent_fractals.verdict: COHERENT - pattern repeats at all three scales
coherent_fractals.gap: Coupling mechanism differs at each scale (competitive/weighted/promotion). M&I theory says coupling should be bidirectional and dynamic

broken_fractals.name: Specificity
broken_fractals.verdict: BREAK at scoring level - query type specificity detected but never propagated to final scoring weights
broken_fractals.fix: Pass QueryType into spreading_activation_retrieve_with_stats() for weight adjustment

broken_fractals.name: Competition
broken_fractals.verdict: BREAK at memory level - competition exists at embedding level but not at memory level
broken_fractals.fix: Add post-reranking memory competition step using existing conflict detection infrastructure

spike_triggers.cause: Storage/retrieval strength distinction is genuinely new to codebase
Memory-level competition paradigm not previously considered
Clinical neuroscience (SEM, N=2050) validates architectural decisions with hard data

overlook_pass.improvement_surface: Highest-impact: D14 (Scoring fidelity) DROPPED revealing debt. Combined with D5+D18 spikes, the top improvement is separating storage/retrieval strength and adding memory-level competition.

application_map.name: Split Storage Strength from Retrieval Strength
application_map.target_function: Memory::importance() -> split into storage_strength() and retrieval_strength()
application_map.implementation_notes: storage_strength = initial_importance + hebbian_boosts (monotonically non-decreasing). retrieval_strength = f(access_count, recency, context_match). Final score = storage_strength * 0.4 + retrieval_strength * 0.6. Requires updating all importance() call sites.

application_map.name: Query-Type-Dependent Hybrid Weights
application_map.implementation_notes: Pass QueryType from classify_query() into spreading_activation_retrieve_with_stats(). Match arm: Attribute|Factual -> (0.20, 0.40, 0.40), Semantic -> (0.60, 0.25, 0.15), Temporal -> (0.35, 0.30, 0.35).

application_map.name: Retrieval-Time Memory Competition
application_map.implementation_notes: After cross-encoder reranking, for top-K: compute pairwise entity overlap using existing CONFLICT_COSINE_THRESHOLD. For conflicting pairs, keep the one with higher retrieval_strength (more recent + more accessed). Use conflict detection infra from constants.rs.

application_map.name: Automatic vs Deliberate Retrieval Modes
application_map.implementation_notes: enum RetrievalMode { Automatic, Deliberate }. Automatic: lower SPREADING_ACTIVATION_THRESHOLD by 50%, skip cross-encoder, expand candidate pool 2x. Deliberate: current behavior.

application_map.name: Directional Hebbian Edges
application_map.implementation_notes: Split edge_strength: f32 into forward_strength: f32 + backward_strength: f32. Migration: forward = backward = current/2. All edge access points need updating. High effort due to widespread edge usage.

application_map.name: Suppressor Detection in Multi-Signal Reranking
application_map.estimated_delta: Diagnostic-only initially; precision +0.01-0.02 if suppressors demoted
application_map.implementation_notes: Compare semantic-only ranking with final combined ranking. Log memories where position flips >5 places. Expose in retrieval_stats for Prometheus/observability.

application_map.name: Memory Health Dimensions
application_map.estimated_delta: No direct MRR impact; provides diagnostic insight for lifecycle decisions
application_map.implementation_notes: MemoryHealth { vulnerability: f32 (1-confidence), inferiority: f32 (1-importance), disconnection: f32 (1-graph_degree/MAX_DEGREE), fragmentation: f32 (1-content_completeness) }. Inform tier promotion: fragmented memories consolidate before promoting.

priority_ranking.reason: Low effort, high MRR delta (+0.03-0.05), fixes specificity fractal break
Medium effort, high precision delta, addresses contradiction retrieval problem
Medium effort, foundational architecture change, enables better decay modeling
Low effort diagnostic, reveals scoring pathologies
High effort, small direct MRR impact, but architecturally elegant

summary.top_insight: The M&I theory's separation of storage strength from retrieval strength (Bjork & Bjork 1992) reveals a fundamental conflation in veld's current importance model. Combined with query-type-dependent pathway weighting and retrieval-time memory competition, this gives a clear 3-step improvement path backed by clinical neuroscience with N=2050 and SEM validation.
